data.description:
## Story S4: Stale-state auto-recovery + `--force` override with cmdline safety

### Description
**What**: When `test-run start` observes a state dir whose `pid` file is missing, or `kill -0` fails, or the cmdline snapshot mismatches, silently clear the state dir and proceed — no error, no user friction. When invoked with `--force`, unconditionally run the SIGTERM → grace → SIGKILL ladder on the recorded PID, but only after confirming the cmdline snapshot matches. If cmdline does not match, refuse with a clear error (guards against `--force` killing unrelated processes after PID reuse).
**Why**: Without auto-recovery, every orchestrator crash leaves a stuck state dir the user has to clear manually. `--force` is the legitimate "I really do want to restart my own run" path; without cmdline safety it becomes a gun pointed at the user's editor after PID reuse.
**Scope**:
- IN: stale-state auto-clear logic, `--force` flag + cmdline-verified termination, error messages for the `--force` refusal case
- OUT: duplicate-launch guard (S3), legacy harness removal (S6)

### Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, a state dir with a dead recorded PID is silently cleared and `test-run start` proceeds without requiring `--force`
  ← Satisfies: "when the launcher sees <state_dir> but <state_dir>/pid is missing, or kill -0 <pid> fails, or cmdline does not match the snapshot, the launcher silently clears the state dir and proceeds without requiring the override flag. No error, no agent friction"
- When this story is complete, `.claude/scripts/dso test-run start --force` terminates the recorded PID via SIGTERM → grace → SIGKILL and starts a new run
  ← Satisfies: "--force unconditionally terminates the recorded PID via the same SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL ladder the kill subcommand uses"
- When this story is complete, `--force` on a state dir whose cmdline does not match the live process refuses with a clear error instead of sending signals to the unrelated process
  ← Satisfies: "refuses to kill a process whose cmdline does not match — returns a clear error"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

### Considerations
- [Security] cmdline-verified kill prevents signalling an arbitrary process under PID reuse — covered by the same cmdline snapshot mechanism as S3
## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a DSO agent, stale test-run state auto-recovers and --force safely restarts with cmdline-verified termination
